key_insight: The paper turns PDE surrogates into structure-preserving reduced models by combining mixed finite elements with Gaussian-process uncertainty, enabling closed-form flux predictions and tractable posterior error estimates.
engineering_relevance: This could provide fast, real-time surrogate solvers for conservation-law PDEs with built-in physical consistency and uncertainty quantification, useful for verification and decision-making.
method: Learn a low-dimensional structure-preserving state space using H(div)–L2 subspaces of Raviart–Thomas and dgP0 elements via a lightweight transformer.
Model inter-volume fluxes with Gaussian process regression formulated as an optimal recovery problem with equality constraints enforcing conservation.
Use a Schur-complement training strategy to obtain closed-form Dirichlet-to-Neumann estimates and posterior uncertainty for quantities of interest.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments showing accurate posterior distributions and their usefulness as surrogate error estimators.
watch_item: The approach depends on specific mixed FEM structure and constrained GP training, so implementation complexity and generalization to more complex PDE settings may be nontrivial.
paper_title: Structure-Preserving Neural Surrogates with Tractable Uncertainty Quantification
